title: Nihility, Helplessness, and Fictional Habitation
subtitle: World-Binding Failure, Binding Fiction, and Exit Fiction
core_claims: Nihility is not the absence of experience, but the failure of intense internal Δϕ to bind to the world.
Helplessness is the state in which the cost of World-Binding is perceived, learned, or structurally imposed as too high to initiate transition.
Fiction is not merely falsehood; it is a fabricated habitation structure through which humans bind to, edit, or exit the world.
primary_distinctions: fiction as falsehood vs fiction as habitation structure
Binding Fiction Score vs Exit Fiction Gate
recommended_use_cases: language and writing systems as binding structures
do_not_use: Do not use as clinical diagnosis.
Do not use to claim that fiction, religion, mathematics, physics, or science is fake.
Do not use Binding Fiction Score as moral hierarchy.
Do not treat Exit Fiction as inferior to Binding Fiction.
